records.ety: [NL. turpethum, fr. Per. tirbid a cathartic, turbad a purgative root. Cf. Turbith.]

defs: The root of Ipomœa Turpethum, a plant of Ceylon, Malabar, and Australia, formerly used in medicine as a purgative; -- sometimes called vegetable turpeth.
A heavy yellow powder, Hg3O2SO4, which consists of a basic mercuric sulphate; -- called also turpeth mineral.
